evals.prompt: Repurpose this podcast into short-form content for Brenda Thompson, all 4 templates, then tell me which method wins. Transcript: docs/Belle & Perry/Transcripts/BT-HSS POD-EP35.md --client brenda-thompson --template all
evals.expected_output: Four candidate-set markdown files under output/brenda-thompson/bt-hss-pod-ep35/ (01-04), each with 3-5 segments and per segment: 5 hooks, 3 A/B captions, 3-5 long-form LinkedIn/FB posts, real timestamps, no em dashes/buzzwords/emojis. Plus a comparison.md scoring all 4 on the rubric with a recommended winner/hybrid.

assertions.text: Produces 4 output files, one per template (01-04)
Each template yields 3-5 segments with real MM:SS timestamps from the transcript
Each segment has exactly 5 hooks, 3 captions, and 3-5 long-form posts
Zero em dashes and zero buzzwords (game-changer/leverage/unlock/dive into/seamlessly) across all output
No 'listen to the full episode' as the only CTA; pieces are self-contained
Template 3 captions/posts genuinely differ across Instagram/LinkedIn/Facebook
comparison.md scores all 4 on the 5 rubric dimensions and names a winner or hybrid

evals.prompt: Run just the marketing-advisor template on docs/Belle & Perry/Transcripts/BT-HSS POD-EP35.md for brenda-thompson.
evals.expected_output: One file output/brenda-thompson/bt-hss-pod-ep35/04-marketing-advisor.md following the output spec, with the template-04 signature: target word-count per segment (~75 words/30s) and a suggested tighter 15-45s in-point, hooks using Mystery/Mistake/Exclusivity/Transformation.

assertions.text: Notes target word density and a tighter in-point per segment
